description: WiringPi GPIO: The industry standard command-line utility for manipulating, evaluating, and controlling General Purpose Input/Output header pins at the system level.

commands.explanation: Overlays a complete visualization grid mapping every physical Board Pin, its matching BCM/wiringPi ID, its Mode (IN/OUT/ALT), and its exact live current Value (0/1).
Forces a designated BCM pin (-g) into an OUTPUT state, structurally preparing it to deliver external electrical logic.
Configures a pin to an INPUT state, structuring it to passively read external voltages supplied by physical sensors/buttons.
Drives the designated output pin HIGH, supplying exactly 3.3 volts of electrical current directly to the header.
Drives the designated output pin LOW, sinking the voltage back to a theoretical absolute 0v grounded state.
Returns the explicit active state integer (0 or 1) presently being perceived on the targeted INPUT physical pin.
Engages the CPU's internal microscopic Pull-Up resistor circuit natively anchoring a floating pin logic to a consistent HIGH state.
Engages the internal Pull-Down resistor effectively draining ungrounded chaotic noise establishing a solid resting LOW state.
Sets specifically supported output pins into Pulse Width Modulation mode enabling analogue-like operations (fading LEDs, running servo motors).
Drives a precise numerical duty cycle down the PWM active pin. Standard ranges fall dynamically from 0 to 1023.
